It is important that how we write the codes and organize them.
In this chapter, we learn how can we write codes to read ez.
First of all we have to Avoid Capital_Alphabet and spaces.
For example
```{R}
#Strive for:
  short_flights <- flights %>% filter(air_time < 60)
#Avoid for:
SHORTFLIGHTS <- fligths %>% filter(air_time < 60)

#Strive for
z <- (a + b)^2 / d

#Avoid for
z<-( a + b ) ^ 2/d

#Strive for 
mean(z, na.rm = TRUE)

#Avoid for
mean (x ,na.rm=TRUE)
```

In the Pipes
```{R}
# Strive for
flights |>
  filter(!is.na(arr_delay), !is.na(tailnum)) |>
  count(dest)

# Avoid
flights|>filter(!is.na(arr_delay), !is.na(tailnum))|>count(dest)
```

Also, if we can not fit all of the arguments to a function on to a single line, put each argument on its own line
```{R}
flights |>
  group_by(dest) |>
  summarize(
    distance = mean(distance),
    speed = mean(distance / air_time, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = distance, y = speed)) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    span = 0.5,
    se = FALSE,
    color = "white",
    linewidth = 4
    ) +
  geom_point()

#Look carfully for " |> " and " + "
```

Exercise
```{R}
flights |>
  filter(dest == "IAH") |>
  group_by(year, month, day) |>
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    delay = mean(arr_delay, na.rm=TRUE)
    ) |> 
  filter( n > 10)
flights |>
  filter(
    carrier == "UA", dest %in% c("IAH", "HOU"),
    sched_dep_time > 0900, 
    sched_arr_time < 2000
    ) |>
  group_by(flight) |>
  summarize(
    delay = mean(
      arr_delay, na.rm = TRUE),
    cancelled = sum(is.na(arr_delay)),
    n=n()
    ) |>
  filter(n > 10)
```
